summary: Ronacher frames 2025 as a personal and professional pivot: he left Sentry, started a company, and shifted from hands-on coding to leading AI agents. He argues that agentic tooling has changed how software is built and how engineers relate to machines, creating both productivity gains and uncomfortable anthropomorphization. He highlights that most debates in this space are driven by vibes rather than evidence, often influenced by incentives. He sees tensions between outsourcing to vendors and the renewed ability to build in-house with AI, and he leans toward simpler, self-built systems. His conclusion is a set of wishes: new version control, review, and observability paradigms that capture prompts, failures, and human intent while making AI work legible and responsible.
key_points: Agentic coding has reshaped his workflow from direct coding to overseeing and steering AI tools.
Current discourse about AI tooling is opinion-heavy and evidence-light, with incentives muddying preferences.
AI enables building core infrastructure internally, yet industry trends push toward outsourcing and dependencies.
Traditional VCS and code review models fail to capture prompts, failed paths, and human intent in AI-assisted work.
LLM-driven observability could unlock richer, more dynamic debugging and analysis workflows.
money_quotes: If you would have told me even just six months ago that I'd prefer being an engineering lead to a virtual programmer intern over hitting the keys myself, I would not have believed it.
Basically: what Claude Code innovated is still state of the art for me.
An LLM with memory is an experience that is hard to shake off.
I increasingly take issue with calling these machines "agents," yet I have no better word for it.
The pull request model on GitHub doesn't carry enough information to review AI generated code properly — I wish I could see the prompts that led to changes.
Code review to me feels like it needs to become part of the VCS.
The better the feedback loop to the machine, the better the results.
I find reading such pull requests quite rage-inducing.
key_insight: Agentic coding boosts leverage but breaks existing tooling and social norms, so the field needs new VCS, review, and observability practices that preserve human accountability and context.
